_status: DRAFT — finalize the TODO fields before computing the frozen hash / tagging prereg-legal-reversal-v1. Freezing placeholders is not a real pre-registration.

corpus.westlaw_role: label-validation-and-taxonomy-reference-only (no bulk extraction; ToS + TR v. ROSS 2025)
corpus.TODO_court_set: e.g. all U.S. Courts of Appeals reviewing U.S. District Court civil merits decisions; fix the exact court IDs
corpus.TODO_year_range: e.g. decisions 1990-2015 (so appellate history has matured by data pull)

splits.rule: temporal; train < t0, validate [t0,t1), test >= t1; no unit in two splits

label.positive: reversed OR vacated in direct appellate history

stratify_by_ground.rationale: the compiler measures MORAL structure; reversal tracks LEGAL error. They align for rights/procedure grounds, not for precedential/statutory ones. Stratify (and pre-commit the primary target = gate-relevant reversals) so the measured pathology matches the label.
stratify_by_ground.primary_target: reversals on gate-aligned grounds (sharper, more uniquely-geometric)

compiler.pipeline: erisml-compiler (MoralGraph -> MoralTensorV3 + projections); IR schema erisml_compiler_ir_v0.3; Zenodo DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20659432
compiler.NOT_aesthetics_compiler: the aesthetics-compiler emits visual dims (color/contrast/closure) and is the WRONG tool; the ErisML compiler emits normative structure
compiler.tensor: MoralTensorV3; rank-2 (k,n) primary [k=moral dimension, n=stakeholder], rank-1 (k,) global fallback. Only ranks 1-2 are wired (higher ranks raise NotImplementedError -- honest contract).
compiler.coordinate_match: these k-dimensions are ~the GDT 9-D decision-manifold coordinates (rights/fairness/autonomy/privacy/legitimacy/epistemic/virtue/social); the compiler is in effect a concrete GDT encoder -- the low-rank pillar and pathology score live in the same space as the rest of the program
compiler.extractor: MoralGraph is built by LLM + spaCy-SRL annotation of the text -- a source of extraction noise/bias; see caveats.extractor
compiler.input_text: court's own opinion, redacted per label.leakage_redaction

pathology_score_S.space: the MoralTensorV3 (GDT-coordinate) representation of each opinion
pathology_score_S.k_rule: smallest k with marginal explained variance < 0.01 (fit on train only, then frozen)
pathology_score_S.TODO_weights: convex weights on the three components, e.g. [0.4, 0.3, 0.3]; fix before test use
pathology_score_S.standardization: z-score S on training split; apply same transform to val/test

H1_reversal.metric: OR per SD of S, selection-corrected

H2_unique.metric: held-out ΔAUC of S over legal-BERT baseline

H3_structure.metric: monotonicity of reversal in off-manifold residual + boundary elevation

selection_control.preferred: random-trial-judge-assignment IV (examiner design) if court set supports it
selection_control.fallback: Heckman two-stage (stage1 P(appeal), stage2 P(reverse|appeal) with inverse-Mills)
selection_control.reporting: raw AND selection-corrected; headline = corrected; H1 requires sign/threshold stable across both

multiplicity: Holm across the 5 confirmatory rows; all else exploratory
TODO_min_test_n: e.g. 20000; do not run confirmatory tests below this
tier_gate: Tier-A success REQUIRES H1 AND H2 AND H3. H1-only-with-H2-fail => reported as Tier B (re-description). Committed, not reframable post hoc.

caveats.moral_vs_legal: the compiler encodes MORAL structure (GDT coordinates), not the full legal apparatus (precedent, statutory interpretation, procedure). Reversals driven by purely legal-technical error are invisible to it -- hence the gate-aligned primary target. A null on all-grounds reversal with a hit on gate-aligned reversal is the expected, honest shape.
caveats.extractor: the MoralGraph is LLM/SRL-extracted, so the geometry partly reflects the extractor's reading. Mitigations: fix the extractor + version before freeze; report inter-extractor stability on a sample; treat the extractor as part of the frozen pipeline.
caveats.single_jurisdiction: one court set / window; positive result is not universal (cross-court replication is future work).
